pi_items.source: Task description (line 7) matches metadata claim: 'Take a screenshot of the 3D view showing the installed ramp and save it to C:\Users\Docker\Documents\ramp_install.png'

No direct evidence found in task scripts (task_utils.ps1, setup scripts for similar tasks) that references this path. The path follows standard Windows AppData conventions but cannot be independently verified from available task infrastructure.

Task description claims 'Sample House' but multiple authoritative sources contradict this: (1) evidence_docs/README.md line 6 states 'Tasks: 5 tasks using the Contemporary House sample project'; (2) evidence_docs/README.md lines 42-43 confirm 'Contemporary House loaded in 3D editor' with window title 'DreamPlan by NCH Software - Contemporary House'; (3) task_utils.ps1 (shared environment setup) consistently references 'Contemporary House' throughout (lines 174, 192, 489, 527, 617, 638, 677); (4) sibling task change_flooring_material/task.json line 5 explicitly mentions 'Contemporary House' sample project

privileged_info_summary: The install_wheelchair_ramp task is designed for the DreamPlan Home Design Software environment running Windows 11. Screenshots should be saved to C:\Users\Docker\Documents\ramp_install.png as confirmed by task description. However, the actual sample project used in the environment is 'Contemporary House' (not 'Sample House' as claimed in metadata). The environment uses Contemporary House for all tasks as documented in the official evidence_docs/README.md and implemented consistently in the task_utils.ps1 shared setup scripts. DreamPlan is installed at C:\Program Files (x86)\NCH Software\DreamPlan\dreamplan.exe with projects stored in the user's AppData directory following standard Windows conventions.
